summary: Docker reproduction confirms authentication bypass through HTTP header spoofing, allowing complete impersonation of admin users and execution of privileged actions without valid credentials.
expected_security_impact: Spoofing authentication headers should grant unauthorized access to admin actions and protected functionality, demonstrating complete authentication bypass.
actual_observed_result: Successfully executed admin-only actions by setting X-Auth-User and X-Auth-Group headers to arbitrary values, creating proof files demonstrating unauthorized action execution.
verification_method: Script sends requests with spoofed authentication headers and verifies creation of proof files from admin-only actions that should require valid admin credentials.
evidence_produced_by_run: Admin actions executed with spoofed X-Auth-User header
Proof files created demonstrating unauthorized access
No validation of header values performed
Complete impersonation of any user possible
ACL checks bypassed through header manipulation

reproduction.source_acquisition: Clones OliveTin, configures AuthHttpHeaderUsername, demonstrates header spoofing
